title: Bart The General

jokes.text: Do I smell cupcakes? - Ooh! Do I ever! -Uh-uh, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer eagerly asks about cupcakes
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately shuts him down with a firm 'Uh-uh'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession meets Marge's practicality - good character work

jokes.text: Keep your greasy mitts outta there
jokes.setup: Homer going for the cupcakes
jokes.punchline: Bart's colorful insult 'greasy mitts'
jokes.explanation: Good character voice for Bart - colorful language that's not quite profanity

jokes.text: You're saying 'buttkisser' like it's a bad thing
jokes.setup: Being scolded for saying 'butt kisser'
jokes.punchline: Bart's mock confusion that it's negative
jokes.explanation: Smart comeback that shows Bart's precocious cynicism about adult values

jokes.text: Well, you see, boy, it never hurts to grease the wheels a little
jokes.setup: Lisa insisting she's not buttering up her teacher
jokes.punchline: Homer encouraging exactly that behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - completely missing the moral point and encouraging corruption

jokes.text: It's the three roads to success, Bart: work, brains and hmm-- Oh, brother.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining three roads to success
jokes.punchline: Can't think of the third one, Marge's exasperated reaction
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - starting confidently then failing, plus Marge's knowing reaction

jokes.text: Doh!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with something (implied physical comedy)
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase that became cultural phenomenon, perfect character integration despite simplicity

jokes.text: My sister here made a whole pile up cupcakes to butter up her teacher, and she won't give anybody else even one measly little crumb
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get Otto on his side
jokes.punchline: Completely misrepresenting Lisa's innocent gesture as calculated manipulation
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart manipulation - spinning an innocent act into something sinister to get what he wants

jokes.text: Here, Otto. I made an extra one for you
jokes.setup: Bart's manipulation attempt
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately undermining him by being genuinely generous
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Lisa's genuine kindness immediately deflating Bart's scheme

jokes.text: You sniveling toad! You little egg sucker!
jokes.setup: Bart frustrated that his manipulation failed
jokes.punchline: Creative insults including 'egg sucker'
jokes.explanation: Creative insults that sound serious but are silly enough for kids' TV

jokes.text: Back-scratcher. Foot-licker. Honor student!
jokes.setup: Continuing his list of insults
jokes.punchline: Honor student delivered as the ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - treating academic achievement as an insult perfectly captures Bart's anti-establishment worldview

jokes.text: In the heat of the moment, I said some things I didn't mean
jokes.setup: Bart trying to apologize to get cupcakes
jokes.punchline: Using adult apology language insincerely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's manipulative side using adult phrases

jokes.text: What do you like best about me? / Well, I'd have to say... your generous nature, your spirit of giving
jokes.setup: Lisa making Bart really work for forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Bart forced to compliment the sister he just insulted
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Lisa getting her revenge by making Bart grovel

jokes.text: Open your mouth and close your eyes, and you will get a big surprise
jokes.setup: Lisa seemingly offering Bart a cupcake
jokes.punchline: Classic trick setup that audience knows won't end well
jokes.explanation: Classic setup that relies on audience familiarity with the trick

jokes.text: Thanks, Lis. You're the best
jokes.setup: Bart presumably got tricked instead of getting cupcake
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic thanks after being had
jokes.explanation: Good character response - Bart's sarcastic acceptance of being outsmarted

jokes.text: Nelson, you're bleeding. / Nah, happens all the time. Somebody else's blood splatters on me
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out Nelson is bleeding
jokes.punchline: Nelson casually explaining it's usually other people's blood on him
jokes.explanation: Excellent character establishment for Nelson - dark humor that establishes him as genuinely dangerous while being funny

jokes.text: Hey, wait a minute. You're right. You made me bleed my own blood!
jokes.setup: Nelson realizing he's actually bleeding
jokes.punchline: Outrage at bleeding his 'own' blood as if that's the unusual part
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Nelson's outrage focuses on the wrong aspect (whose blood it is rather than that he's bleeding)

jokes.text: Uh-oh. A cold wind
jokes.setup: Principal Skinner approaching
jokes.punchline: Bart's poetic description of impending doom
jokes.explanation: Nice atmospheric moment - Bart's poetic way of describing trouble approaching

jokes.text: No, no, no. He'll get you after school, son. Now hurry up. It's time for class
jokes.setup: Bart trying to protest the fight arrangement
jokes.punchline: Authority figure casually enabling playground violence
jokes.explanation: Good satire of school authority - Principal treating scheduled violence as normal procedure

jokes.text: Lunchtime! Ha, ha, ha! - Lunchtime, Bart. It's lunchtime.
jokes.setup: Nelson's menacing 'lunchtime' threat
jokes.punchline: Bart's terrified repetition
jokes.explanation: Establishes Nelson's intimidation style and Bart's fear. 'Lunchtime' as euphemism for beating is mildly clever.

jokes.text: I ain't gonna get out of the fourth grade alive
jokes.setup: Bart's fear about the upcoming fight
jokes.punchline: Treating elementary school like a war zone
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - treating elementary school with war movie gravitas

jokes.text: It would violate the code of the schoolyard
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggesting telling the principal
jokes.punchline: Bart treating playground rules like sacred law
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - elevating playground rules to constitutional status

jokes.text: Here comes my brother, Bart the bully-killer!
jokes.setup: Kids thinking Bart won the fight
jokes.punchline: Lisa's enthusiastic but wrong assessment
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the misunderstanding of what actually happened

jokes.text: I'm not sayin' I'm not a hero. I'm just saying that I fear for my safety
jokes.setup: Being hailed as a hero
jokes.punchline: Bart's diplomatic way of accepting praise while acknowledging he's doomed
jokes.explanation: Good Bart logic - wanting the glory while acknowledging the reality

jokes.text: Good-bye, little dude. He looks so lifelike, man
jokes.setup: Bart's 'funeral'
jokes.punchline: Talking about Bart like he's dead while he's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - treating a living person's 'funeral' as real

jokes.text: Who wants a day off from work when I'm never gonna see my beloved son again?
jokes.setup: Homer initially happy about getting off work
jokes.punchline: Forced to pretend sadness about losing Bart
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his priorities (work vs family) briefly showing before he corrects himself

jokes.text: I know you can't eat it now, so I'll just place it lovingly on your forehead
jokes.setup: Lisa feeling guilty about the cupcake situation
jokes.punchline: Placing food on 'dead' Bart's forehead as tribute
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist visual - the bizarre funeral ritual with food

jokes.text: Hey, look. They got food at this thing
jokes.setup: Solemn funeral atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on the refreshments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - completely inappropriate priorities at emotional moments

jokes.text: Oh, man, that guy's tough to love
jokes.setup: Nelson scheduling another beating
jokes.punchline: Bart's understated assessment of Nelson's character
jokes.explanation: Good understated response to extreme situation - very Bart-like

jokes.text: Let's just say I paid the inevitable price for helping out my sister
jokes.setup: Parents asking about his injuries
jokes.punchline: Bart's melodramatic way of explaining getting beat up
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Bart using overly dramatic language to describe simple events

jokes.text: I'm gonna miss you, big guy
jokes.setup: Bart asking for help
jokes.punchline: Homer already mourning him instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - defeatist rather than helpful when his kid needs support

jokes.text: I'm gonna miss you, big guy.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Bart needing help
jokes.punchline: Immediately assuming Bart will die rather than helping
jokes.explanation: Homer's defeatist parenting - skipping straight to mourning

jokes.text: The code of the schoolyard, Marge. The rules that teach a boy to be a man. Let's see. Don't tattle. Always make fun of those different from you. Never say anything unless you're sure everyone feels exactly the same way you do
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the 'code of the schoolyard'
jokes.punchline: List of terrible life lessons presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire - Homer earnestly listing genuinely toxic masculinity lessons as if they're wisdom. Sharp social commentary.

jokes.text: This bully friend of yours, is he a little on the chunky side? / Yeah, he's pretty chunkified, all right.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Nelson's appearance
jokes.punchline: Bart's invented word 'chunkified'
jokes.explanation: Bart's creative language - 'chunkified' is a memorable made-up word

jokes.text: Well, thank you very much, Mrs. Maharishi Gandhi
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting understanding and communication
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic mashup of spiritual leaders
jokes.explanation: Good Homer sarcasm - combining spiritual leaders into one dismissive reference

jokes.text: Unfortunately, son, we Simpsons sometimes have to bend the rules a little in order to hold our own
jokes.setup: Teaching Bart to fight
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically describing cheating as 'bending rules'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - justifying cheating with family pride rhetoric

jokes.text: That little doozy's been a Simpson trademark for generations
jokes.setup: Teaching about hitting someone in the groin
jokes.punchline: Treating dirty fighting as proud family tradition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 'family jewels' mention, treating crude fighting as heritage

jokes.text: Remember the family jewels, son.
jokes.setup: Bart heading into another fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's parting advice about hitting below the belt
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's fighting advice

jokes.text: Remember the fight he put up when we put him in the home?
jokes.setup: Marge saying Grandpa is the toughest Simpson
jokes.punchline: His 'toughness' was resisting being institutionalized
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elder care - reframing resistance to nursing home as 'toughness'

jokes.text: We are not all vibrant, fun-loving sex maniacs. Many of us are bitter, resentful individuals who remember the good old days when entertainment was bland and inoffensive
jokes.setup: Grandpa writing complaint letter about TV
jokes.punchline: Self-aware description of elderly stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - Grandpa's self-aware complaint that's both funny and accurate social commentary

jokes.text: We are not all vibrant, fun-loving sex maniacs. Many of us are bitter, resentful individuals who remember the good old days when entertainment was bland and inoffensive.
jokes.setup: Grandpa writing complaint letter about TV portrayal of elderly
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as bitter and preferring bland entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Grandpa advocating against positive stereotypes while embodying negative ones

jokes.text: Number one: bra. Number two: horny. Number three: family jewels
jokes.setup: List of words he doesn't want to hear on TV
jokes.punchline: Includes 'family jewels' which was just discussed
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects Grandpa's prudishness with the earlier crude fighting advice

jokes.text: My arm? Well, let me put it this way. Next time your teacher tells you to keep your arm inside the bus window, you do it!
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Herman lost his arm in the war
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a mundane school bus accident
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - building up war heroism then deflating with mundane childhood accident

jokes.text: The key to Springfield has always been Elm Street. The Greeks knew it. The Carthaginians knew it. Now you know it
jokes.setup: Herman giving military strategy advice
jokes.punchline: Treating suburban street like ancient battlefield
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - applying grand historical military strategy to elementary school conflicts

jokes.text: I can't promise you victory. I can't promise you good times. But the one thing I do know-- Whoa, whoa! All right! Okay! I promise you victory! I promise you good times!
jokes.setup: Bart giving realistic military pep talk
jokes.punchline: Immediately changing tune when kids object
jokes.explanation: Good leadership comedy - Bart's quick pivot from realism to crowd-pleasing

jokes.text: You can push them out of a plane, you can march them off a cliff, you can send 'em off to die on some godforsaken rock, but for some reason, you can't slap 'em
jokes.setup: Herman having to apologize for hitting a kid
jokes.punchline: List of extreme violence that would be okay vs. mild slapping
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - escalating list of extreme violence contrasted with mild discipline being forbidden

jokes.text: Well, I'd rather they say 'Death From Above,' but I guess we're stuck
jokes.setup: Water balloons that say 'Happy Birthday'
jokes.punchline: Bart preferring menacing military terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart getting into the military role-play

jokes.text: It's a classic pincers movement. It can't fail against a ten-year-old
jokes.setup: Herman explaining battle strategy
jokes.punchline: Applying serious military tactics to child opponent
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist contrast - serious military strategy against elementary school bully

jokes.text: I thought I'd never hear the screams of pain or see the look of terror in a young man's eyes. Thank heaven for children
jokes.setup: Herman feeling rejuvenated by the conflict
jokes.punchline: Thanking heaven for children as outlets for violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes Herman as genuinely disturbed character while being funny

jokes.text: Ha! Oh, yeah? You and what army? / This one.
jokes.setup: Nelson's classic taunt
jokes.punchline: Bart literally having an army
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline - taking the rhetorical question literally with perfect timing

jokes.text: There are no good wars, with the following exceptions: The American Revolution, World War I, and the Star Wars trilogy
jokes.setup: Anti-war message about there being no good wars
jokes.punchline: Immediately listing exceptions including fictional space opera
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of PSA format - undermining anti-war message immediately, treating fiction as history

tmdb_overview: Terrorized by the school bully, Bart seeks counsel from Grandpa Simpson, who takes him to a warfare expert for basic training.
insight: Early Simpsons balances dark war satire with character comedy at solid 76.8, anchored by 50 jokes spread thin.
